page: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU mapped to an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX_VNNI, and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates placing in the top tier. However, due to its single shared vCPU configuration, multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the lower performance tiers. Memory performance is also constrained, with PassMark memory scores in the bottom decile and read-only memory bandwidth categorized as weak.

This server offers a highly cost-efficient profile for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads where single-threaded execution speed is prioritized over parallel processing. The shared CPU allocation and limited memory capacity make it less suitable for heavy database operations or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for basic general-purpose tasks, lightweight background workers, development environments, and simple single-threaded applications.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-2GB is a budget-friendly, entry-level virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM and 25 GB of local SSD storage. While its single-core performance is highly competitive—achieving top-tier results in single-threaded compression and stress-ng benchmarks—its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are significantly limited due to the single shared vCPU architecture. This resource profile makes the server a cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, development environments, and basic utility tasks, rather than demanding database or high-throughput web serving applications.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-2GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2 GB RAM, and 25 GB SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-2GB is a KVM virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2 GB RAM, and 25 GB SSD, optimized for basic single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance, 2 GB RAM, and local SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPU at 2.0 GHz
2.0 GB system memory and 25 GB local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression benchmarks
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for lightweight utility tasks and development environments
